mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hike periods with t-stat > 2.0

mechanism_narrative: A rapid quarterly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances when unemployment rises by more than 0.5 percentage points

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer discretionary and fixed investment sectors, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to produce a statistically significant positive mean return (p > 0.05) over 20-day horizons

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects improving capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum, which translates into higher short-term equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are non-positive in > 55% of periods where industrial production growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation readings above 0.4% force central banks to accelerate monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are positive in > 60% of months where inflation exceeds 0.4% month-over-month

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor market expansion and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean during periods of positive payroll growth with t-stat < -1.5

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP growth and retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion across macro aggregates, which boosts earnings forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns fail to exceed baseline by > 0.5% when both GDP and retail sales show simultaneous acceleration

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing activity, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances where industrial production declines by more than 0.3% month-over-month
